_note: New York/NJ HQ data — MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford NJ. Most matches of any host city (8).

venue.name: MetLife Stadium
venue.address: One MetLife Stadium Dr, East Rutherford, NJ 07073
venue.notes: Largest venue in the tournament at 82,500 capacity. Clear bag policy, no re-entry. Gates open 2.5 hours before kickoff.
venue.parkingNote: Extensive on-site parking starting at $60. Reserve via FIFA app or StubHub. NJ Transit is strongly preferred — parking traffic post-match can add 60+ minutes.

transit.recommended: NJ Transit — Meadowlands Rail Line from Penn Station (direct, ~15 min ride, runs only on event days)
lines: NJ Transit Bus 320 from Port Authority
tips: The Meadowlands Rail Line runs direct from Penn Station on event days — easiest option from Manhattan.
Buy your return ticket before the match — queues at station are long post-game.
PATH train to Hoboken + Shuttle is a good backup option. Ask at Hoboken Terminal.
From Brooklyn/Queens: LIRR to Penn then Meadowlands Rail. Budget 75 min total.
transit.parking: On-site lots open 5+ hours before kickoff. $60–$90 via official app. Blue 1 lot closest to main entrance.
transit.rideshare: Designated Uber/Lyft pickup on the east service road, Gate C area. Heavy surge pricing expected — consider NJ Transit back and rideshare from Penn Station.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Flushing Meadows Corona Park
fanZones.notes: Official FIFA fan zone. Giant screens, entertainment, food from around the world. Accessible via 7 train.

fanZones.name: Hudson Yards Fan Hub
fanZones.notes: Unofficial but massive gathering point near Penn Station before Meadowlands Rail. Multiple bars and screens.

preGame.name: Hoboken bar crawl
preGame.distance: PATH from Manhattan; NJ Transit bus to stadium
preGame.notes: Hoboken's Washington St corridor is electric on match days. Marathon Bar, 1 Republik, and Green Rock fill up fast.

preGame.name: Hell's Kitchen soccer bars
preGame.distance: 10 min walk to Penn Station, then Meadowlands Rail
preGame.notes: Legends NYC, Smithfield, and Nevada Smiths (E Village) are NYC's top soccer bars. Arrive 2+ hours early.

preGame.name: Flushing Meadows Fan Festival
preGame.notes: Best pre-game energy if you want the official FIFA experience. Large, family-friendly, international food.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Brazil vs Morocco — opening weekend at the biggest venue. NYC's Brazilian community is massive and vocal. Expect 80,000+ in gold.
Japan vs Tunisia — Japan's New York diaspora is large and organized. Expect a colorful, disciplined fan section.
Brazil vs Scotland — Brazil in MetLife is the loudest match in the city. Scotland fans travel in massive numbers and create unforgettable atmosphere.
Switzerland vs Qatar — technical match with Group B implications. Quieter crowd but still MetLife scale.
Brazil vs Haiti — NYC's Haitian community will be enormous here. Brazil nearly guaranteed to advance; still a carnival.
Japan vs Sweden — final group-stage weekend. Both teams likely fighting for knockout spots. High stakes.
Brazil knockout match — if Brazil are here it's already a party. MetLife at full roar for a Round of 32.
Japan knockout match — Blue Samurai fans are impeccably organized. Expect a dramatic night at MetLife.

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Brazil vs Morocco opening weekend — maximum crowds. NJ Transit will be packed. Book return rail ticket in advance.
tips: Take Meadowlands Rail — driving is chaos
Buy round-trip rail ticket before you leave Manhattan
Arrive at Penn Station 45 min before train

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Japan vs Tunisia — evening start, manageable compared to Brazil matches. NJ Transit runs smoothly.
tips: Evening kickoff — avoid NJ Transit during 5pm rush
Arrive by 6pm for 7pm kickoff to beat the rush
Post-match transit back to Penn is straightforward

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Brazil vs Scotland — Scottish fans + Brazilian diaspora at MetLife. Expect full stadium and full transit stress.
tips: This is one of the most anticipated matches of the tournament at this venue
Do not drive — Meadowlands Rail is your only realistic option
Scottish fans will sing all night — budget for late return

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Switzerland vs Qatar — smaller crowd than Brazil/Scotland matches. Easiest transit day of MetLife's schedule.
tips: Most manageable MetLife match — good if it's your first visit to the stadium
Transit runs on time; post-match queue at station is short

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Brazil vs Haiti — NYC Haitian community turns out in full. Lively crowd, significant transit pressure.
tips: Caribbean fan communities are vocal and celebratory — great atmosphere
Meadowlands Rail required — parking will be full by noon

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Japan vs Sweden — Group F finale, potential must-win for both teams. Evening match, normal transit pressure.
tips: Final group stage match — Japan and Sweden may both need points
Arrive 90 min early; stadium fills early for high-stakes finales

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Brazil knockout at MetLife — maximum crowd, maximum transit pressure. This is one of the biggest events in New York history.
tips: Plan your entire day around this match
Meadowlands Rail — no other option
Book your hotel/accommodation near Penn Station in advance

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Japan knockout — intense match, organized Japanese fan sections, evening atmosphere at MetLife.
tips: Japan fans are famously respectful; expect orderly transit queues from their section
Evening knockout energy is electric — MetLife at night is unforgettable

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil vs Morocco at the world's biggest World Cup venue. 82,500 fans, opening weekend, NYC energy at full blast. This is a once-in-a-lifetime event in your city.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Japan vs Tunisia — exciting group stage football, great value tickets, strong fan bases from NYC's Japanese community.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil vs Scotland — the most atmospheric group-stage match in New York. Scottish fans are legendary. NYC's Brazilian community plus 80,000 others make this historic.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Switzerland vs Qatar — best ticket value of MetLife's schedule. Good football, manageable crowds. Worth it if you want the stadium experience without the chaos.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil vs Haiti — NYC Haitian and Brazilian communities make this electric. Brazil likely through; festive atmosphere guaranteed.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Japan vs Sweden with Group F stakes — likely a must-win situation. High drama, strong traveling support from both sides.

shouldIGoScores.label: Once in a lifetime
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil knockout at the largest World Cup venue on earth. 82,500 people in New York. Nothing in sports comes close to this.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Japan knockout at MetLife — Blue Samurai in elimination football, NYC's Japanese community at full presence. Memorable night.
